rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. MICHAEL E. MCGINTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons. Granted. The burden of proof is on the plaintiff to prove the facts requisite to an effective service. (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 313.) Although Evidence Code §647 creates a presumption that the facts contained within a process server's affidavit are true, Defendant rebutted this presumption by providing evidence that no one was home when the substitute service allegedly occurred. Plaintiff has not met its burden of proof in showing that substitute service was properly effectuated. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DISCOVER BANK VS. MICHAEL E. MCGINTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 1, DEFENDANT MICHAEL MCGINTY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT. Granted. Pursuant to CCP §473(d), the Court may set aside any void judgment. Lack of personal jurisdiction renders a default judgment void, so that it may be vacated at any time. (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) Because Defendant was not properly served and has not generally appeared, the Court lacks personal jurisdiction and the default judgment is void. (See CCP §410.50(a).) Defendant's request for a penalty against Plaintiff under CCP §473(c)(1)(A) is denied. There is no basis for any such penalty here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION INC., VS. PARMINDER S. CHAHAL INDIVIDUALLY DBA MONTEREY MINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT PARMINDER S. CHAHAL INDIVIDUALLY DBA MONTEREY MINI MART's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. CCP §473(b) requires that an application to set aside default "shall be accompanied by a copy of the answer or other pleading proposed to be filed therein, otherwise the application shall not be granted." Plaintiff did not attach a responsive pleading to his motion. rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA NAVARRETE et al VS. ERIC CHUN KHUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 1: Plaintiffs VICTORIA NAVARRETE'S and FERNANDO NAVARRETE'S Motion To Reopen Discovery For Sole Purpose To Compel Production Or Bar The Use Of Surveillance Evidence Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff VICTORIA NAVARRETE and FERNANDO NAVARRETE's Motion to Reopen Discovery for the sole purpose of allowing Plaintiffs to make a motion to compel production or bar the use of Defendants' surveillance is GRANTED. Moving party to submit a proposed Order for the Court's signature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. RMF ENGINEERING, INC., A MARYLAND CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Leave To File Amendment To First Amended X-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 11, CROSS COMPLAINANT RANGER PIPELINES, INC.'s Motion For Leave To File Amendment To First Amended Cross-Complaint. Hearing required. Judge Miller discloses that from 1997 until 2005 when she was appointed to the bench, she was a partner at Morrison & Foerster LLP and worked in the San Francisco office. Judge Miller does not know any of the Morrison & Foerster attorneys listed on the proof of service in this case. This will not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial in this case. The Court is inclined to grant the motion for leave to file amendment to first amended cross complaint. No opposition was filed. A hearing is required so that the moving party may clarify why this motion is on calendar when the Court received a proposed recommended order from the Special Master that appears to be granting the same relief. (See October 2, 2013 letter from John Peterson, case manager for Special Sabraw, attaching "[Recommended] Order Granting Leave to File Amendment to Ranger Pipeline, Inc.'s First Amended Cross Complaint to Substitute True Names for Fictitious Names.".) rulings.Case Title: JANE ROE VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Entry Of Protective Order Req. For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5000.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NICOLE DESANTIS' Motion For Entry Of Protective Order; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $5000.00. Granted, in part. The Court is inclined to sign the proposed protective order submitted with the moving papers except as to the following sections which appear to be in dispute. Section 3(c): no automatic hold for 10 days. Section 2: redefine "producing parties" so that it only includes the parties to this case, not third parties. Section 15: delete provisions requiring disclosure of names of experts, waiting period, meet and confer requirement. Section 11: Clarify that the protective order does not entitle parties to use ex parte applications or modify the Rules of Court concerning such applications. Finally, the Protective Order governs pre-trial discovery, and does not govern the use of documents or testimony at trial. Requests for sanctions are denied. If there are specific areas of dispute between the parties that are not addressed in this tentative ruling, counsel are requested to notify the Court by email, as set forth in the remainder of this tentative ruling. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. J &J. LEASING GROUP, INC. AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 17, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT DOUGLAS BRAY's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion to set aside and vacate default is granted pursuant to CCP §473(d). Defendant Bray has not made a general appearance in this action. Failure to bring a motion to quash does not affect a nonresident defendant's right to challenge a default for lack of personal jurisdiction. The default can be challenged by a motion under CCP §473(d). (Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1250-1251) (motion to set aside for lack of jurisdiction was not affected by nonresident's failure to bring motion to quash.) (See also Rutter Group, Civil Procedure Before Trial, 3:165.6.) Plaintiff has not met its burden of showing that Defendant has sufficient minimum contacts with California so the assertion of jurisdiction does not violate "traditional notions of fair play and substantial justice." (Vons Companies, Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 Cal.4th 434, 444.) Therefore, the default entered in this case is void for lack of jurisdiction under CCP §473(d). Defendant's excusable neglect argument in the alternative does not amount to a general appearance because Defendant's motion, read in its entirety, does not recognize the authority of the court to proceed. (Hamilton v. Asbestos Corp., Ltd. (2000) 22 Cal.4th 1127, 1147.) The Court does not reach that argument in light of the ruling on CCP §473(d). Defendant's motion for sanctions under CCP §473(c)(1)(A) is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
